page: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include any GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2938.58, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance is average, scoring 23323.12 on stress-ng and 18066.14 on PassMark. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only large block speeds at 123.16 GB/sec and write speeds at 72.22 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, it achieves an average static web serving throughput of 8.57 GB/sec and handles 15.55 million Redis SET operations per second. However, prompt processing for medium 7B LLM models is a relative bottleneck, registering a weak score of 25.18 tokens/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-effective option for compute-leaning workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The inclusion of 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage adds value for disk-heavy operations. It is well-suited for web serving, basic database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and lightweight containerized applications.
description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an x86_64 server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (4 physical cores, 2 threads per core) clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier. The server handles static web serving at 8.57 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 15.55 million operations per second, though it exhibits weaker performance in medium LLM prompt processing. This shared-CPU instance provides a budget-friendly, compute-oriented resource allocation suitable for web serving, lightweight databases, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 8 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd features 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced, budget-friendly compute workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-CPU instance pairing eight AMD EPYC cores with fast NVMe storage for web serving and lightweight database workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Suitable for web serving, Redis databases, and multi-threaded decompression
